Fern Por-rrrcAt- Pnncrrces CoMMrssroN
428 J Street e Suite 620 o Sacramento. CA 95g14-2329
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322_0886
October 6, 2008
R. Zachary Wasserman
Wendel, Rosen, Black & Dean LLp
I I 1 1 Broadway, 24th Floor
Oakland, California 94607 -4036
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-08-165
Dear Mr. Wasserman:
This letter relPonds to your request for aclvice regarding the campaign contributions
provisions of the Political Reform Act (the ..Act"). | 
-
QUESTTON
May funds previously transferred to the "Brown 2010 Committee" (established for
2010 Attomey General reelection campaign) be transferred to a new political committee that
may be formed in support of Jerry Brown should he decide to run for Governor in 2010?
CONCLUSION
Yes. Section 85306 permits the transfer of funds from one controlled committee to
a controlled committee for elective state office of the same candidate. The transferred
funds must be attributed to contributors to the Brown 2010 Committee as required by
Section 85306 and Regulation 18536 and are subject to the contribution limiis speciiied in
Section 85301(c) and Section 85302(c) and Regr"rlation 1g545.
FACTS
You represent the Brown 2010 Committee, a committee formed by Attorney General
Jerry Brown to sllpport his reelection in 2010. You wish to know if ftrnds previousiy
' The Political Refirrm Act is contained in Government Code Sections Iil000 thftrugh gl0l4. All
statutory ref'erences are to the Gtlvernment Code. unless otherwise indicated. The regulationi of the Fair
Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections l8I l0 through l8gg7 of Title 2 of the Calitirrnia Code
of Regulations. All regulatory ref'erences are to Title 2. Division r of itre Calitirrnia Cpde ef Regulations.
unless otherwise indicated.


File No. A-08-165
Page No.2
transferred to this committee may be transferred to a new political committee that the
Attorney General may form, if he decides to run for Governor.
ln20o7, funds were transferred from the "Brown for Attorney General Committee,"
which had been formed to support Jerry Brown's candidacy for Attorney General in the 2006
elections, to the Brown 2010 Committee. Because the funds were designated for the same
elective office, no "last in, first out" ("LIFO") or a "first in, first out" (';FIFO") determination
for these contributions was made.2 However, some of the funds that were transferred to the
Brown for Attorney General Committee had previously been designated as "first in, last ont',
because they were transferred from a Brown for Mayor committee.
ANALYSIS
Section 85306(a) states:
"(a) A candidate may transfer campaign funds from one controlled
committee to a controlled committee for elective state office of the same
candidate. contributions transferred shall be attributed to specific
contributors using a "last in, first out" or "first in, first out" accounting
method, and these attributed contributions when aggregated with all other
contributions from the same contributor may not exceed the limits set forth in
Section 85301 or 85302."
While it would appear that, under the above section, attribution would be required for
all transfers a candidate makes from one of his or her committees to his or her committee for
elective state office, Section 85317 eliminates the attribution requirement if the contributions
are being carried over between committees for the same elective state office. It states:
"Notwithstanding subdivision (a) of Section 95306, a candidate for
elective state office may carry over contributions raised in connection
with one election for elective state office to pay campaign
expenditures incurred in connection with a subsequent election for the
same elective state office."
Therefore, it was not necessary to do the attribution to conttibutors required under
Section 85306 for the transfer of funds from the Brown for Attorney General bommittee of
2006 to the Brown 2010 Committee for Attorney General, because the committees are for the
same elective state office.
With respect to the potential transfer of funds from Brown 2010 Committee for
Attomey General to a committee in sLlpport of Jeny Brown's possible run for Governor in
t Yorr letter indicated that no "'last in. flrst out' or 'first in, last out"'determination was made fbr
these contributions. We assume you meant no "last in, first out" or a "t'irst in. tlrst out" as there is no .'first in,
last out" option tbr candidates fbr elective state otfice. (Section g5306(a).)


File No. A-08-165
Page No. 3
2010, Section 85306 would apply; the funds may be transferred and are subject to the
attribution requirements set forth in that section and in Regulation 1g536.
Regulation 18536 (copy enclosed) outlines the Commission's rules pertaining to the
transferring and attribution of contributions and provides in pertinent part:
"(a) A committee transferring funds must designate in its record at the
time of its first transfer whether it elects the 'first in, first out' or a .last in,
first out' method of accounting for the current and future transfers. That
designation is irrevocable.
OD
"(3) Campaign funds shall be attributed to contributors in the lesser of the
following amount:
"(A) The actual amount of the original contribution from the person to
whom the campaign funds are being attributed;,'
"(B) The applicable contribution limit under Govemmenr code [s]ection
85301 or 85302; or
"(c) The amount of campaign funds the committee is seeking to transfer
that has not yet been attributed."
Therefore, the Brown 2010 Attorney General committee may use either the LIFO or
FIFO method and transfer funds to a20IO committee for Governor.l Ho*ever, funds may
not be transferred, if the funds attributed to a specific contributor exceed the contribution
limits of Section 85301 or 85302 when aggregated with all other transfers attributed to, and
contributions from, the same contributor.
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Scott Hallabrin
dJ)eral
y: Will
Lenkeit
Senior
, Legal Division
wJLjgl
Enclosure
r The committee does not have to use the same method used when the tunds were transf-erred from the
Brown fur Mayor Committee to the 2006 Brown fbr Attorney General Committee.

QUESTION: May funds previously transferred to the "Brown 2010 Committee" (established for
2010 Attomey General reelection campaign) be transferred to a new political committee that
may be formed in support of Jerry Brown should he decide to run for Governor in 2010?

CONCLUSION: Yes. Section 85306 permits the transfer of funds from one controlled committee to
a controlled committee for elective state office of the same candidate. The transferred
funds must be attributed to contributors to the Brown 2010 Committee as required by
Section 85306 and Regulation 18536 and are subject to the contribution limiis speciiied in
Section 85301(c) and Section 85302(c) and Regr"rlation 1g545.
